Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Neural Network Inference
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the curvature of the loss landscape can be used to guide precision routing in neural networks, leading to improved efficiency and accuracy. By leveraging curvature-aware diagnostics, we can identify regions of the loss landscape where high precision is required and adaptively allocate precision accordingly.
Related Work: Our proposal is related to recent works on spectral quantization routing and spectral latent steering, which have shown the effectiveness of spectral methods in optimizing neural network inference. However, our approach differs in that we focus on curvature-aware precision routing, which has not been explored in previous works. We also draw inspiration from geometric signal separation and training-free geometric interference cancellation, which have demonstrated the importance of geometric methods in robustifying neural network inference.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient neural network inference. Our approach leverages curvature-aware diagnostics to identify regions of the loss landscape where high precision is required and adaptively allocates precision accordingly. We theoretically ground our framework in PAC-Bayes bounds, which link curvature to generalization stability under quantization noise. We evaluate our approach on single-GPU setups across various benchmarks and demonstrate a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines.
Experiments: Evaluate the effectiveness of curvature-aware precision routing on various neural network architectures and benchmarks
Compare the performance of our approach with static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines
Investigate the impact of curvature-aware diagnostics on precision allocation and inference efficiency
Analyze the trade-off between precision and accuracy in our framework
Risk Factors and Limitations: The curse of dimensionality may limit the applicability of our approach to high-dimensional datasets
The computational overhead of curvature-aware diagnostics may be significant for large neural networks
The choice of curvature metric and precision allocation strategy may require careful tuning for optimal performance
